avalancheActivity.highlights: In places where a lot of snow falls danger level 4 (high) will be reached in the course of the day.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Over a wide area 40 to 60 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, will fall until the evening above approximately 1900 m.
The snowpack will become unstable in particular on steep slopes.

As a consequence of new snow and a moderate to strong wind from southeasterly directions, precarious wind slabs will form in the course of the day in particular in gullies and bowls as well as above the tree line. On steep slopes and on wind-loaded slopes the likelihood of avalanches is high.

The sleet will give rise to moistening of the snowpack below approximately 1800 m. At lower altitudes and on very steep slopes small to medium-sized gliding avalanches and moist snow slides are possible as the penetration by moisture increases.

snowpackStructure.comment:
40 to 60 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, will fall until the evening above approximately 1900 m.
The large quantity of fresh snow and the wind slabs that are forming during the snowfall will be deposited on a crust in particular on sunny slopes above approximately 2000 m. They are bonding poorly with the old snowpack at high altitude.

tendency.highlights: The natural avalanche activity will increase.

avalancheActivity.comment:
Over a wide area 20 to 30 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, will fall until the evening above approximately 1900 m.
As a consequence of new snow and a sometimes moderate wind from northeasterly directions, mostly small wind slabs will form in the course of the day. The fresh wind slabs will be covered with new snow and therefore difficult to recognise.

The fresh snow and the wind slabs formed during the snowfall can be released by a single winter sport participant. This applies in particular on steep slopes at intermediate and high altitudes, as well as in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain.

The sleet will give rise to moistening of the snowpack below approximately 1800 m. In particular on very steep grassy slopes and in extremely steep terrain small to medium-sized gliding avalanches and moist snow slides are possible as the penetration by moisture increases.

snowpackStructure.comment:
40 to 60 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, will fall until the evening above approximately 1900 m.
The fresh snow and the mostly small wind slabs that are forming at high altitudes and in high Alpine regions will be deposited on a crust in particular on sunny slopes above approximately 2000 m.
The fresh snow will be rather moist. Especially in areas with smaller amounts of fresh snow, this will favour bonding with the old snowpack.

The sleet will give rise to moistening of the snowpack below approximately 1800 m.

tendency.highlights: Medium-sized natural avalanches are possible on very steep slopes.

avalancheActivity.highlights: He avalanche danger will increase in the afternoon.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Over a wide area 20 to 30 cm of snow, and up to 40 cm in some localities, will fall until the evening above approximately 1900 m.
The new snow will become progressively more unstable at intermediate and high altitudes, especially on steep slopes.
Avalanches can in some places be released, even by a single winter sport participant and reach medium size.

The sleet will give rise to moistening of the snowpack below approximately 1800 m.

snowpackStructure.comment:
20 to 30 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, will fall today above approximately 1900 m.
The fresh snow and the mostly small wind slabs that are forming at high altitudes and in high Alpine regions will be deposited on a crust in particular on sunny slopes above approximately 2000 m.
The fresh snow will be rather moist. Especially in areas with smaller amounts of fresh snow, this will favour bonding with the old snowpack.

The sleet will give rise to moistening of the snowpack below approximately 1800 m.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Moist avalanches below approximately 1900 m. New snow at elevated altitudes.
avalancheActivity.comment:
10 to 20 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, will fall until the evening above approximately 1900 m.
The rain will give rise to moistening of the snowpack below approximately 1800 m.
Isolated mostly small dry and moist avalanches are possible as a consequence of the new snow. Avalanches can in some places be released, in particular by large loads.
In regions exposed to heavier precipitation and at elevated altitudes the avalanche prone locations are more widespread and the danger is greater.

snowpackStructure.comment:
The sleet will give rise to moistening of the snowpack below approximately 1800 m.

The fresh snow will be rather moist. This snow will be deposited on a crust in particular on sunny slopes. Especially in areas with smaller amounts of fresh snow, this will favour bonding with the old snowpack.
